candidate.headline: NASA selects seven companies for STRIDE Mars robotic-mobility study contracts
candidate.note: Flagged via Marcia Smith's Bluesky post. NASA's STRIDE program awarded design-study contracts (total ~$17M, up to $3M per company) to 7 U.S. companies for advanced Mars surface-mobility concepts, work targeted to begin fall 2026.

held.reason: Scope question: Mars-exploration technology study contracts to commercial companies. Has a commercial-provider angle (7 companies funded) but reads as planetary-science procurement via small design-study awards, closer to the 2026-07-05-Q ESA Aeolus-2 precedent (legacy institutional science procurement excluded despite a commercial prime) than to new-space commercial-market activity (EO/connectivity/launch/human spaceflight). Flagging for Florian rather than unilaterally publishing or discarding.

candidate.headline: New Singapore space agency seeks to build up the country's space industry

held.reason: Institutional agency-to-agency cooperation (Singapore's NSAS and Japan's JAXA signed a memorandum of cooperation July 6). The commercial angle is aspirational ('hope to grow it a bit more in the next few years') with only one company (SpeQtral) quoted reacting positively; no contract, program, or market-access specifics stated. Same shape as the NATO HALO (2026-07-08-H2) and Aeolus-2 (2026-07-05-Q) precedents; a genuine scope question for Florian rather than a unilateral publish or discard.
